name: Lisbon Portuguese
notes: Lisbon prestige variety. Diagnostic features per DIALECT_PATTERNS / whitepaper5 (TigreGotico internal dialect research): (1) /ej/ → [ɐj] — Lisbon diphthong lowering, the single most diagnostic grapheme-level feature (leite→[lɐjtɨ], primeiro→[pɾiˈmɐjɾu]); (2) /ow/ → [o] — complete monophthongization (ouro→[ˈoɾu]); (3) unstressed /a/ → [ɐ] throughout; (4) unstressed /o/ → [u] (pretonic/posttonic); (5) strong unstressed /e/ → [ɨ] (inherited from pt-PT base); (6) consistent uvular [ʁ]; (7) coda /l/ → [ɫ] (inherited). Allophonic spirantization (β/ð) is phonemizer-level and not encoded here. Sources: DIALECT_PATTERNS.md, whitepaper5 §3 (Lisbon de-biasing / §5.3 Lisbon profile), Mateus & d'Andrade (2000).

sources.author: TigreGotico
sources.title: DIALECT_PATTERNS.md and whitepaper5: Phoneme-Level Dialect Transforms for European Portuguese
sources.notes: Internal dialect feature matrix and IPA transform inventory; pending external peer review
